fact: October 18 is exactly 291 days into the year, which means there are only 74 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single season and forget where half of them are!
The sun is setting almost 2 minutes earlier each day around October 18 in most places, making bedtime feel like it comes super fast.
October 18 falls smack in the middle of Libra season, which means if you're born today, your zodiac symbol is a scale - the only zodiac sign that's not a living thing!
Maple trees are dumping their leaves like crazy right now in cold places, and a single big maple can drop over 600,000 leaves in one fall season!
Why do spiders seem to be everywhere in mid-October? Because this is mating season for house spiders, so they're running around looking for partners!
The moon phase on October 18 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it's called a Hunter's Moon - and it looks extra orange and huge!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, October 18 is prime baby animal season - lambs, calves, and joeys are being born as spring heats up!
Morning frost starts appearing on car windshields around this time in many northern places, creating those cool ice crystal patterns that look like tiny ferns.
October 18 is roughly 28 days after the autumn equinox, which means daylight and darkness are getting seriously unbalanced - darkness is winning!
Woolly bear caterpillars are crawling everywhere right now, looking for cozy spots to hibernate - and people think their brown stripes can predict how harsh winter will be!
